our forever,
so far.

three years in, and it still feels like the good kind of chaos. to the one i'll love forever, in every life. every photo here is a moment together, matched with our stupid humour. tap one, hear it back.

us, so far

till london

a little something

I was all noise before you running full speed at nothing mistaking loud and chaotic for alive. Reckless the way young fellas are reckless convinced I knew everything, built for chaos, allergic to being still. Then you. You didn't fix me, love was never the tool for that, you just stood close enough that I wanted to be better you made the better version of me feel worth actually becoming. You honed edges I didn't know I had, Spoke to me gently, which somehow lands harder than any shouting or speech ever did. I'm still me. Still rough round the edges, And with site dust under my nails, still chasing the big dream too loud for most rooms. But I'm better rough now, sanded down where it mattered. That's you. Years of just you, staying, when staying wasn't the easy road. You make me 17 and 26 in the same breath. reckless enough to still believe my dreams, steady enough now to actually build the ladder up to it, because you're the one holding the other end of the rope, the same as you always have been. We made up our own language because English wasn't built for what I feel about you. Megan, you never tamed me (impossible) You just gave the wildness somewhere worth going. Happy anniversary. I can't wait for London for a door that's ours, a bed that's ours, waking up next to my best friend in a new exciting place here's to forever not as a promise I'm making, but as a place I already know I live.
